title: Nonlinear semigroups with unbounded generators under Carleman linearization
authors: Sitanshu Gakkhar, Ala Shayeghi, David C. Del Rey Fernández
abstract: We treat the convergence of Carleman linearization of nonlinear evolutionary equations through the approximation theory of strongly continuous semigroups, by Carleman embedding the underlying nonlinear semigroups as linear semigroups. Linear semigroup theory then lets one replace the norm constraint on the convergence of Carleman linearization in the form used by quantum algorithms for a class of semi-discretized evolution equations by a dissipativity constraint, simplifying arguments for convergence. Applying Trotter-Kato approximation theorem to the linearized semigroup realizes the semigroup as a limit finite dimensional operator exponentials, reducing the question of convergence rate of Carleman linearization to that of the Trotter-Kato approximation. We then examine convergence of the Carleman linearization as the operators become unbounded, treating the hyperviscuous Burger's equation as an example. Next we consider the perturbation theory of the Carleman semigroup and obtain conditions when polynomial nonlinearities correspond to the Carleman linearized semigroup being a $1$-integrated semigroup, so convergence is implied by variants of Trotter-Kato approximation for integrated semigroups.
summary: This paper provides a rigorous functional analytic framework for the convergence of Carleman linearization, particularly for systems with unbounded operators. It replaces restrictive norm-based convergence conditions with more flexible dissipativity constraints. This allows for the valid approximation of complex nonlinear PDEs using higher-order linear hierarchies.
main_problem: Establishing the convergence and well-posedness of Carleman linearization for nonlinear evolutionary equations, especially when the underlying operators are unbounded, such as in discretized partial differential equations.
main_result: The authors prove that the convergence of truncated Carleman hierarchies can be reduced to the convergence of the Trotter-Kato approximation and establish conditions for the generation of C0-semigroups and 1-integrated semigroups using dissipativity constraints.
method: The study employs the approximation theory of strongly continuous semigroups, the Trotter-Kato approximation theorem, and Lumer-Phillips theorem, treating the nonlinear dynamics as a linear system on a free Fock space.
model_or_system: Nonlinear polynomial evolutionary equations of the form phi' = W1*phi + W2*phi^2, with specific applications to the hyperviscous Burgers' equation and reaction-diffusion type equations.
key_observables: The solution u(t) to the nonlinear equation, recovered by projecting the solution of the linearized system onto the first tensor factor.
important_parameters: The Carleman truncation level N, the nonlinearity parameter R, the dissipativity of the linear operator W1, and the relative A-bound of the perturbation.
main_assumptions: Assumes the linear part W1 is a closed, densely defined, and dissipative operator; requires the generators to be closable; and assumes certain kernel inclusions for the nonlinear terms.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the Carleman embedding in Fock space, moves to establishing convergence via Trotter-Kato and dissipativity analysis, addresses the unbounded operator regime using relative boundedness, and concludes with specific examples like the hyperviscous Burgers' equation.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant for researchers developing quantum algorithms for nonlinear dynamics, as it provides a rigorous mathematical foundation for the convergence of Carleman-based classical simulations of nonlinear systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
